figures.caption: Figure 4: Ablation study measuring the upper-bound contribution of each information factor.
Table 4: Combination of two factors. Roughly sorted by contribution.

Table 5: Details of validation experiment result, including performance gain for each factor compared to the weaker model baseline and average cost of the two stages. Labels are formatted as Benchmark: (strong model) + (weak model).
Table 3: Contribution of execution context for error stack and custom stack respectively.
Figure 5: Accumulative contribution and average number of rollout steps when factors accumulate. Each factor on the x-axis incorporates all factors to its left.
Figure 8: Average number of steps for single factors.
Figure 9: Average cost of rollout for single factors.
Figure 1: Extraction pipeline of oracle information signals for Code Search, including Edit Location, Execution Context and API Usage.
Table 1: The factors each work focuses on in Related Works.
Figure 7: (a) Proportion of SWE agents’ exploration pattern, taken directly from (Bouzenia & Pradel, 2025). (b) Proportion of SWE-Agent’s failure modes, taken directly from (Yang et al., 2024).
Figure 6: Visualized result of the two-stage validation compared with the single-agent baseline. Labels are formatted as (strong model) + (weak model).
Figure 2: Extraction pipeline of Oracle Tests, including Reproduction Test and Regression Test.
Figure 10: Accumulative contribution and average cost with factors accumulated from another direction.
